summary: Reporter passes a .lottie file (dotLottie ZIP archive) to SkiaSharp.Skottie.Animation.Create(), but Skottie only supports plain Lottie JSON — the ZIP data fails JSON parsing silently and returns null.

stepsToReproduce: Observe that the returned Animation is null

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: The Skottie API has never supported dotLottie ZIP format — this is not version-specific.

analysis.summary: The .lottie format (dotLottie) is a ZIP archive containing Lottie JSON inside an /animations/ directory plus a manifest.json. Skia's Skottie module only parses plain Lottie JSON. When Animation.Create() reads a .lottie file, it reads raw ZIP bytes, strips BOM, and passes them to skottie_animation_make_from_data() which attempts JSON parsing on binary ZIP data and fails silently, returning null. This is arguably by-design since dotLottie is a separate format, but the silent failure and lack of documentation about format support make it a valid usability bug.
analysis.rationale: Classified as type/bug with low confidence because it's borderline between bug and feature-request. The Skottie API doesn't claim dotLottie support, but the silent null return without error information is a poor developer experience. The reporter reasonably expected .lottie files to work since LottieFiles distributes them as a standard format. Severity is low because there's a simple workaround (extract JSON from ZIP) and the API is working as implemented.

keySignals.interpretation: Reporter is passing a .lottie file path directly to Animation.Create, expecting it to handle the dotLottie format.

keySignals.text: A null animation instance is returned instead
keySignals.interpretation: Silent failure — no exception, no diagnostic, just null. This is the correct C# behavior for factory-null-on-failure, but the root cause is format incompatibility.
LottieFiles download page — default download format is .lottie (ZIP), not .json. Users must explicitly choose JSON format.

codeInvestigation.finding: Create(string path) reads the file as raw bytes via SKData.Create(path), then delegates to TryCreate(SKData) which strips BOM and passes raw bytes to skottie_animation_make_from_data(). No file extension check, no ZIP detection.
TryCreate(SKData) strips UTF BOM preamble then passes raw bytes to skottie_animation_make_from_data(). For ZIP data, this passes binary ZIP bytes as if they were JSON text, which silently fails.

skottie_animation_make_from_data calls skottie::Animation::Make(data, length) which expects JSON text. Returns nullptr for non-JSON input — this becomes IntPtr.Zero in C# and then null from GetObject().

workarounds: Download the Lottie animation as .json instead of .lottie from LottieFiles (use the JSON download option)
Extract the JSON from the .lottie ZIP file programmatically using System.IO.Compression before passing to Animation.Create/Parse
nextQuestions: Should SkiaSharp add native dotLottie ZIP extraction support, or is this out of scope?
Does upstream Skia's Skottie module have any dotLottie support planned?

resolution.hypothesis: The reporter is using a .lottie (dotLottie) file which is a ZIP archive, but Skia's Skottie module only parses plain Lottie JSON. The null return is expected for non-JSON input.

proposals.title: Download as JSON instead of .lottie
proposals.description: On LottieFiles, use the JSON download option instead of .lottie. The .lottie format is a ZIP archive that SkiaSharp does not support.

proposals.title: Extract JSON from .lottie ZIP programmatically
proposals.description: Use System.IO.Compression to unzip the .lottie file and extract the animation JSON from the /animations/ directory inside the archive, then pass the JSON string to Animation.Parse().

proposals.title: Add dotLottie format detection in Animation.Create
proposals.description: Add ZIP magic number detection (PK header: 0x50 0x4B) in TryCreate(SKData) to automatically extract and parse the contained JSON. This would be an enhancement to the SkiaSharp binding layer.

resolution.recommendedReason: Simplest immediate fix — no code changes required. The .lottie format is a separate container format that Skia's Skottie module doesn't support.

actionability.reason: Skottie only supports plain Lottie JSON format. The .lottie (dotLottie) ZIP format is a separate container format not supported by Skia. However, better error messaging or documentation would improve the developer experience.

actions.description: Labels are already correct — type/bug, area/SkiaSharp, os/Windows-Classic, tenet/reliability
Explain dotLottie vs Lottie JSON format difference and provide workaround
